options.name: --preview
options.description: Preview generated .gitignore without writing to disk

options.name: --merge
options.description: Merge with existing .gitignore (append unique patterns only)

options.name: --add
options.description:
Add custom patterns (e.g., --add "*.log" "tmp/" "secrets/")
Notes:
When inspecting a project, a .snapshot.json file is saved alongside the .txt report.
This snapshot can later be used with --from-report to track structural drift over time.
The gitignore command detects all project ecosystems (including nested/hybrid projects)
and NEVER overwrites an existing .gitignore — it creates a .gitignore.generated_* file instead.
Use --merge to safely append missing patterns to an existing .gitignore.
Use 'verify' to run security and compatibility checks without any access keys.

commands.name: open
commands.description: Open an existing report with the default viewer

options.name: --open
options.description: Open the report automatically after saving
